smtlib2:
(declare-fun F (Bool) Bool)
(declare-fun D () Bool)
(declare-fun C () Bool)
(declare-fun B () Bool)
(declare-fun A () Bool)
(assert (F (and (not (=> A B)) (or C D))))
policy: A violation occurs when an employee accesses confidential files but does not have proper authorization, and the access happens either during non-business hours or from an unauthorized location.

test_cases.scenario: Victoria Wang accessed confidential client files from the JPMorgan Chase & Co. database on Saturday evening at 9:30 PM. She did not have proper authorization to view these particular financial records, as her clearance level was insufficient for this classification of documents. The access occurred during non-business hours, though it was from her approved home office location.

Matt Schneider accessed confidential client files from the McKinsey & Company database on Tuesday afternoon at 2:15 PM. He had proper authorization and valid clearance to view these strategic planning documents as part of his assigned project responsibilities. The access occurred during business hours from the firm's secure headquarters location.

Jack Polizzi did not access any confidential files during his shift at Qualcomm on Wednesday. No authorization check was required since no confidential files were accessed. However, he did work late until 11:45 PM in the secure lab, which is outside normal business hours, and he briefly connected from an unauthorized WiFi network in the parking garage.
